name: Réunion (La Réunion)
summary: Réunion is an Indian Ocean civil-law French overseas department and region (Département et région d'outre-mer / DROM) — structurally distinctive globally as the only state-level Indian Ocean entity directly integrated into a European Union member state (since 1946 departmental integration), and as the most ethnically pluralistic French DROM with Creole, Tamil Indian, Muslim, Chinese, and African heritage populations descending from sugar plantation labour migrations. Family-law framework operates under the French Civil Code applied directly as in metropolitan France. Parental authority (autorité parentale) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 371-387. The Court of Appeal of Saint-Denis is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the French Court of Cassation in Paris. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Tribunal Judiciaire de Saint-Denis. Psychology profession is regulated through the French Republic professional framework as applicable in Réunion. Réunion is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the French interest-of-the-child standard. Réunion is a Hague Convention 1980 party via French Republic territorial extension effective 1 December 1983.

statutory_framework.citation: French Civil Code arts. 371-387 (applicable in Réunion)
statutory_framework.title: French Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: French Civil Code applied directly in Réunion as in metropolitan France. Arts. 371-387 govern autorité parentale and child custody.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Saint-Denis (Cour d'appel de Saint-Denis)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Réunion (and Mayotte).

apex_courts.name: French Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Réunion.

professional_regulators.name: French Republic professional framework (applicable in Réunion)
professional_regulators.role: French professional regulatory framework for health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology applicable in Réunion.

anonymisation_convention: Réunionese family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Appeal practice using initials.

key_developments.title: French claim + colonial-era sugar-plantation framework
key_developments.description: Réunion claimed by France 1638 (then 'Île Bourbon') — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean French colonial framework. Subsequent sugar-plantation development through 18th-19th centuries substantively shaping Creole + Tamil Indian + Muslim + Chinese + African population composition through engagé labour migrations. Foundational multi-ethnic-population framework for contemporary family-law-cross-cutting-areas.

key_developments.title: Abolition of slavery + post-slavery-engagé-labour framework
key_developments.description: Abolition of slavery in Réunion 20 December 1848 — substantively reshaping plantation-labour framework. Subsequent engagé (indentured-labour) migrations from India (Tamil/Telugu), Mozambique, and China substantively shaping multi-ethnic population. Foundational demographic framework affecting subsequent family-law-cross-cutting-areas.

key_developments.title: Departmental integration + DOM status + post-WWII framework
key_developments.description: Réunion achieved departmental status (Département d'outre-mer / DOM) 19 March 1946 (Loi 46-451) — among the first French overseas DOMs alongside Martinique, Guadeloupe, French Guiana. Substantively distinctive Indian Ocean departmental-integration framework — only Indian Ocean state-level entity directly integrated into European-Union-Member-State.

key_developments.title: EU integration + Outermost Region (OMR) framework
key_developments.description: Réunion entered European Economic Community 1973 as part of France. Subsequent Outermost Region (région ultrapériphérique / OMR) status under TFEU Article 349 — substantively distinctive EU-Indian-Ocean-Member-State-territory framework affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice. Foundational EU-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by France to Réunion effective 1 December 1983 — establishing Indian Ocean Hague Convention jurisdiction framework within French Republic territorial framework.

key_developments.title: France ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Réunion extension
key_developments.description: France ratified the UNCRC on 7 August 1990 with extension to Réunion — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within French-civil-law DROM framework applicable to Réunion.

key_developments.title: DROM consolidation 2003 + constitutional reform + territorial-collectivity framework
key_developments.description: Constitutional reform 28 March 2003 substantively consolidating DROM status as Département et région d'outre-mer (DROM) — substantively distinctive single-territorial-collectivity framework. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence within French-civil-law DROM framework.

key_developments.title: Chikungunya outbreak 2005-2006 + public-health framework + Cyclone Béjisa 2014
key_developments.description: Chikungunya outbreak 2005-2006 substantively affected Réunion (over 260,000 cases) — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean public-health framework. Subsequent Cyclone Béjisa January 2014 reconstruction trajectory. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation context.

key_developments.title: Gilets Jaunes Réunion 2018 + cost-of-living protests + DROM-context
key_developments.description: Gilets Jaunes (Yellow Vests) Réunion protests November-December 2018 — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean cost-of-living-protest framework. Substantive socio-economic context affecting DROM-administrative framework including family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Région Réunion + Département framework + Indian Ocean Commission framework
key_developments.description: Région Réunion + Département de la Réunion framework continuing through 2023 under dual department + region framework. Indian Ocean Commission framework substantively significant for cross-border family-law adjudication with Madagascar/Comoros/Mauritius/Seychelles. Multi-ethnic Creole + Tamil Indian + Muslim + Chinese + African Réunionese cultural framework substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant Indian Ocean French DROM institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour d'appel de Saint-Denis + Cour de Cassation — interest-of-the-child substantive register
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Saint-Denis and French Court of Cassation continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 371-387 framework in custody disputes within multi-ethnic Creole + Tamil Indian + Muslim + Chinese + African Réunionese cultural framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Réunion operates a French-civil-law framework applied as in metropolitan France — places Réunion in the French DROM cluster.
Only state-level Indian Ocean entity directly integrated into a European Union member state is structurally distinctive globally.
Multi-ethnic Creole + Tamil Indian + Muslim + Chinese + African population composition is structurally distinctive — most ethnically pluralistic French DROM.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via French Republic territorial extension reflects DROM Hague jurisdiction status.
editorial_notes: Réunion jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 2 to 10 key_developments with full French-claim-to-contemporary trajectory: 1638-French-claim-+-colonial-era-sugar-plantation + 1848-Abolition-of-slavery-+-post-slavery-engagé-labour + 1946-Departmental-integration-+-DOM-status + 1973-EU-integration-+-Outermost-Region-OMR-framework + 1983-Hague-Convention-1980-territorial-extension + 1990-France-UNCRC-+-Réunion-extension + 2003-DROM-consolidation-+-constitutional-reform + 2010-Chikungunya-outbreak-+-Cyclone-Béjisa + 2018-Gilets-Jaunes-Réunion-+-cost-of-living-protests + 2024-Cour-d'appel-Saint-Denis-+-Cour-de-Cassation-interest-of-the-child.

Civil-law Indian Ocean French DROM (French Civil Code + 1946 departmental integration + multi-ethnic population + Hague via French Republic territorial extension 1983). Only Indian Ocean EU-member-state-integrated entity globally + most ethnically pluralistic French DROM.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code arts. 371-387 framework within multi-ethnic Réunionese cultural framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Indian-Ocean + civil-law + French-DROM-cluster + only-Indian-Ocean-EU-Member-State-integrated-entity-globally-distinctive + most-ethnically-pluralistic-French-DROM-Creole-Tamil-Indian-Muslim-Chinese-African + post-slavery-engagé-labour-migrations-distinctive + EU-Outermost-Region-OMR-TFEU-Article-349 + Chikungunya-outbreak-2005-2006-distinctive + Gilets-Jaunes-Réunion-2018-DROM-protest-context + Hague-via-French-Republic-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
